proposed_from.topic: Sharp partial identification and Neyman-orthogonal root-n inference for a NON-SEPARABLE policy-relevant functional of the joint gain law Y(1)-Y(0) (focal: the two-sided gain-band psi=E_omega[1{a<=Y(1)-Y(0)<=b}], the policy-affected share whose gain falls in a band, and the induced gain-dispersion index) in the generalized Roy/MTE model of arXiv 2604.12263 with a discrete IV. The anchor's 1-D closed-form quantile-coupling reduction and DML both rest on linearity-in-y; for the non-supermodular band cost separability is lost, so the sharp identified set is the value of a rank-structured constrained OT / linear-fractional LP (Charnes-Cooper) with an analyst-specified copula-sensitivity restriction R(eps) on the finite joint-outcome grid, and inference is rebuilt on the LP-dual optimizer as the Neyman-orthogonal influence function (root-n + asymptotic normality under dual uniqueness; Fang-Santos directional bootstrap otherwise). Extends Rem general_functional / Rem marx-open.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"Failed pid_ot_dte_marginal/v1 isolated the pasting obstruction: locally feasible response-type cells need not belong to one full-data law. Failed pid_poc_tau_band/concordance_sharp_dr left interval-band attainment and score control unresolved. This kernel makes the corrected factual map, one-law pasting, and the dual-tie split explicit and uses neither an association-loss sign frontier nor a cosmetic restatement of those proposals.","published_axis":"The strict contribution is a common-coupling sharpness question for a non-product gain-band cost under Roy rank and polyhedral copula restrictions. The class \\mathcal W_\\kappa shows that its target set cannot be represented by separable/product-cost or assembled marginal bounds. Existing generalized-Roy, MTE, generic MOT, and OT-inference results inform different pieces but do not jointly deliver the factual-map, common-law, nonseparable endpoint, and regular/nonregular inference characterization."}

accepted_scope_caveats.label: thm:dual-inference
accepted_scope_caveats.caveat: Inference is conditional on a repaired and affirmatively resolved sharp common-coupling characterization and on the stated regular finite-LP regime.
accepted_scope_caveats.bound_claim: Root-n and directional-bootstrap claims apply only conditional on the repaired sharp finite-grid generalized-Roy identified-set result.

banked_reason: Ordinary extension of the anchor paper with insufficient contribution for the field novelty target.
